trigger: Correct the first Team Admin sign-in route so users stay inside the ResearchOps UI and authenticate through a ResearchOps-owned passwordless email-code flow.
request_interpreted: Do not expose Cloudflare sign-in screens, routes or implementation language to the user.
Let the user enter their email address in ResearchOps.
Let the user enter a one-time code in ResearchOps.
Use D1 for users, identities, teams, roles, permissions, login challenges, sessions and audit.
Use the Worker as the authentication and authorisation boundary.
Use Airtable only after Worker authorisation.
Continue to identify Team Admin capability through role.assign.
findings: The previous sign-in implementation was written from a system perspective and exposed Cloudflare Access language to the user.
The clarified requirement matches the product document's passwordless email magic link or code method rather than a visible Cloudflare Access handoff.
The existing D1 auth foundation already supports users, identities, teams, roles, permissions, role assignments and audit events.
The missing pieces were app-owned login challenges, app-owned sessions, Worker endpoints for start/verify/logout, and UI forms for email/code entry.
The Team Admin role continues to map to role.assign and the role-assignment UI remains the post-login continuation route.
implemented_flow: POST /api/auth/email/start creates an auth_login_challenges record and sends a 6 digit code.
POST /api/auth/email/verify verifies the code, creates or links the D1 user identity, creates an auth_sessions record and sets an HTTP-only ResearchOps session cookie.
GET /api/me resolves the ResearchOps session before falling back to Cloudflare Access JWT support.
The sign-in page shows Manage team roles only when /api/me returns role.assign.
security_boundary: The page does not enforce authorisation.
The page only collects email and code and renders account state.
The Worker validates codes and sessions.
D1 stores hashed code and session values rather than plaintext secrets.
D1 remains the canonical store for user, identity, team, role, permission and audit state.
No password fields, localStorage or sessionStorage are introduced.
Cloudflare-branded sign-in pages are not exposed to the user in this flow.
validation_encoded: The sign-in page collects email in ResearchOps.
The sign-in page collects a 6 digit code in ResearchOps.
The page does not expose Cloudflare sign-in language.
The passwordless server module uses D1 challenge and session tables.
The canonical auth resolver prefers ResearchOps sessions before Cloudflare Access fallback.
The Team Admin continuation route remains /pages/team/role-assignments/.
